gaps.live_evidence: "Primesti : 159 credite" (Complex Mediteranean, 7993 Lei), "Primesti : 203 credite" (Savoy), "Primesti : 43 credite" (Astoria); also the inline nudge "Stiai ca ... poti primi credite cadou daca faci rezervarea fiind logat in contul tau"
gaps.redesign_evidence:
`
+ 4 credite FRIENDS / noapte
`
gaps.recommendation: Show the stay total as the primary figure ("Primești 159 credite FRIENDS pentru acest sejur") and optionally the per-night rate as secondary. Add the Lei equivalent if the conversion is fixed.

gaps.feature: Named commercial programmes on cards ("Oferta Verii", "Oferta Last Minute", "Zile Gratuite de Vacanta", "Litoralul Pentru Toti", "Extra Discount", "Long Stay") including stay conditions
gaps.why_it_matters: These are LITRO's own negotiated charter campaigns with years of media spend and brand recall in Romania — "Litoralul pentru toți" in particular is a nationally recognised programme. The live cards also carry the conditions inline ("minim 5 nopti", "minim 3 nopti", "plata integrala", "Nerambursabila"). The redesign collapses all of it into two generic pills, so the campaign equity disappears and the qualifying conditions only surface later — a classic late-disclosure drop-off at checkout.
gaps.live_evidence: "Oferta Verii", "Zile Gratuite de Vacanta", "Oferta Speciala a Verii Flexibil 20.07.2026 minim 5 nopti - Oferta Extra discount plata integrala 20.07.2026", "Oferta Standard 2026 minim 3 nopti", "Oferta Speciala Nerambursabila 2026 (MD)"; top nav also lists "Oferte litoral / Last minute / Oferta Verii / Litoralul Pentru Toti / Extra Discount"
gaps.redesign_evidence: `Înscrieri Timpurii` and `O ofertă specială disponibilă`
gaps.recommendation: Render the real programme name in the pill (branded per campaign) and put the binding condition as a short caption under the price ("minim 5 nopți · plată integrală · nerambursabil"). Keep the campaign entry points in the header nav too.

gaps.feature: Full facilities filter block (~50 amenities with counts)
gaps.why_it_matters: Romanian seaside demand segments hard on amenities the redesign has no way to express: "Baza tratament" (balneo/treatment base — a whole customer segment and a state-voucher use case), "Piscina pentru copii", "Loc de joaca", "Patut tip tarc pentru bebelus", "Adults Only", "Piscina interioara", "Spa", "Priza de incarcare masini electrice", "Rampa de acces persoane cu dizabilitati". Cutting from ~50 filters to 6 chips with no overflow forces those users to scan 81 cards manually or leave. On a one-coast, 500-property inventory, amenity filtering IS the primary differentiation axis — there is no geography to filter on.
gaps.live_evidence: "Facilitati" block lists ~50 entries with counts: All Inclusive (11), Piscina (26), Plaja privata (35), Baza tratament (2), Piscina pentru copii (17), Loc de joaca (31), Spa (10), Adults Only (1), Patut tip tarc pentru bebelus (37), Camera pentru persoane cu dizabilitati (11), Self check-in (1), Sauna (11), Jacuzzi (9) …
gaps.redesign_evidence: `Filtre populare` fbox with exactly 6 chips: Piscină (84), Mic dejun inclus (197), Pe plajă (52), Parcare (143), Aer condiționat (228), Animale acceptate (61) — no "Vezi toate" / overflow
gaps.recommendation: Keep the 6-chip "Filtre populare" as the fast path but add "Vezi toate facilitățile (48)" opening a grouped, searchable panel (Piscine / Familie / Wellness & tratament / Accesibilitate / Servicii). Also surface "All inclusive" and "Plajă privată" as first-class filters — both are core LITRO charter propositions absent from the rail.

gaps.feature: Destination aggregate review score ("Hotelurile din Mamaia au o medie de 8.7 puncte din 10, din 11395 recenzii") and the site-wide 9.0 equivalent
gaps.why_it_matters: 11 395 reviews is LITRO's proof that it is not a thin affiliate feed — it is first-party volume accumulated over 20+ years of actually operating these stays. That single number is the cheapest available answer to "why book here instead of Booking.com", and it also props up every individual property score below it. The redesign replaces it with an unsourced assertion of own inventory.
gaps.live_evidence: "Hotelurile din Mamaia au o medie de 8.7 puncte din 10 / din 11395 recenzii"; footer "Hotelurile de pe Litoralul Romanesc au o medie de 9.0 puncte din 10"
gaps.redesign_evidence: `inventar propriu, confirmare instantanee` — no aggregate score, no review volume
gaps.recommendation: Put the destination aggregate next to the H1/result count: "Cazare Mamaia · 81 cazări · 8.7/10 din 11 395 recenzii verificate" and repeat the site-wide figure in the footer trust strip.

gaps.feature: Result count and unit-type taxonomy match real inventory
gaps.why_it_matters: The live search returns "81 unitati de cazare in Mamaia" split "Hoteluri (70) / Apartamente (7) / Camere (4)". The redesign header claims "319 rezultate" and the rail invents "Vile și case de vacanță (72)", "Pensiuni (37)", "Apartamente (84)", "2 stele (17)" — categories and volumes that do not exist in LITRO's Mamaia allotment. If those filter rows ship, most return zero results and the site looks broken; more importantly the whole layout has been sized for marketplace-scale inventory that a 500-property charter operator does not have. Facet counts must be generated from real data before any of this is signed off.
gaps.live_evidence: "81 unitati de cazare in Mamaia"; "Tipul unitatii: Hoteluri (70) / Apartamente (7) / Camere (4)"; stars: (1)/(29)/(45)/(6)
gaps.redesign_evidence: `319 rezultate`; `Vile și case de vacanță (72)`, `Pensiuni (37)`, `Apartamente (84)`
gaps.recommendation: Re-derive every facet from production data, drop unit types LITRO does not contract in that destination, and hide zero-count rows entirely. Validate that the layout still reads well at 81 results, not 319.

gaps.feature: Review-classification filter labels aligned with the card labels
gaps.why_it_matters: Live uses one vocabulary end-to-end — "Exceptional (48) / Foarte Bine (28) / Satisfacator (5)" in the rail and the same words on cards. The redesign filters on numeric bands (9.5+/9.0+/8.0+) while cards say "Excelent / Foarte bine / Bine", so a user filtering "Excelent 9.5+" gets cards labelled "Excelent" at 9.2. Small, but it makes the scoring system look unreliable, and score credibility is load-bearing when the score is your main trust asset.
gaps.live_evidence: "Clasificare reviews: Exceptional (48) / Foarte Bine (28) / Satisfacator (5)" matching card labels "Exceptional", "Foarte Bine"
gaps.redesign_evidence: Rail: `Excelent 9.5+ (38)`, `Foarte bine 9.0+ (102)`, `Bine 8.0+ (187)`; cards: `Excelent` at 9.2, `Foarte bine` at 8.0
gaps.recommendation: Pick one band definition and use identical words plus thresholds in rail, cards and hotel page. Keep the numeric threshold visible in the filter, it is a genuine improvement — just make the card label derive from the same function.

gaps.feature: Map of the destination including tourist objectives
gaps.why_it_matters: Live offers a real map layer combining hotels and "obiectivele turistice de pe Litoralul Romanesc". On a single coastal strip where every property is 10–570 m from the same beach, micro-location (Cazino vs Nord vs Satul de Vacanță, proximity to Aqua Magic / telegondolă) is the main differentiator a shopper reasons about. The redesign reduces the map to a decorative grey placeholder with a button, and each card links out to a separate map view instead of a synchronised listing↔map interaction.
gaps.live_evidence: "Harta hotelurilor din Mamaia / Si a obiectivelor turistice de pe Litoralul Romanesc"
gaps.redesign_evidence:
`
` — static placeholder; per-card `Vezi pe hartă` links
gaps.recommendation: Specify a real map mode: pins priced, hover-sync with the result list, and a POI layer (plajă, telegondolă, Aqua Magic, Cazino) since micro-location is the primary comparison axis on one coast.

gaps.feature: Legal / operator identity footer (travel agency licence, ANPC, Ministry of Tourism, GDPR registry, T&C, tourist contract)
gaps.why_it_matters: LITRO is the organising travel agency (agenție organizatoare), not a listing platform. Romanian consumer and tourism law requires the licence and identification details plus ANPC contact to be reachable from the site; the tourist contract and T&C must be accessible before booking. The redesign footer is a single copyright line. This cannot launch as-is regardless of design intent.
gaps.live_evidence: "Agentia Organizatoare LitoralulRomanesc.ro / S.C. Creative Eye.S.R.L. / Nr. ROC: J13/2215/2003 / Cod fiscal: RO15613488 / Adresa: Constanta, Bulevardul Mamaia, nr. 158 …"; "Autoritatea nationala pentru protectia consumatorului Telefon: 021 - 9551"; "Directia de control - Ministerul turismului: 080 086 8282"; "Termeni si conditii / Contractul cu turistul / Politica de confidentialitate / Intrebari frecvente"
gaps.redesign_evidence: `© 2008–2026 Litoralul Romanesc · Parte din Szallas Group. Toate drepturile rezervate.` — nothing else
gaps.recommendation: Restore a full legal footer: operator entity + ROC + CUI + address, ANPC and Ministry of Tourism contacts, GDPR registry number, and links to Termeni și condiții, Contractul cu turistul, Politica de confidențialitate, Întrebări frecvente. Treat as launch-blocking sign-off with legal.

gaps.feature: Cookie consent banner / cookie preferences
gaps.why_it_matters: GDPR/ePrivacy consent for analytics and personalised advertising cookies is mandatory before those scripts fire. Live has a compliant banner with granular options. If the redesign ships without it, either the site breaks the law or marketing tags get blocked — and LITRO's paid acquisition depends on those tags.
gaps.live_evidence: "Acest website folosește cookie-uri … folosim și cookie-uri analitice și de publicitate personalizată (inclusiv de la terți) … MODUL COOKIE / ACCEPTA TOT / DOAR NECESARE"
gaps.redesign_evidence: none found
gaps.recommendation: Add the consent layer to the design system (banner + preferences modal), styled to the new tokens, and show it in the spec so it is not treated as an engineering afterthought that lands unstyled.

gaps.feature: Installments messaging on the listing ("Vacante fara griji in 6 rate fara dobanda") and cancellation insurance / holiday-voucher entry points
gaps.why_it_matters: A 7-night Mamaia stay in the live data ranges 2 150–12 985 Lei. Interest-free instalments are a genuine affordability unlock at those tickets and the live site advertises them on the listing page itself, at the moment of price shock. The same applies to Edenred/Up/Pluxee holiday vouchers, a large share of Romanian seaside payment volume. The redesign has a voucher-brand filter but never states that instalments, storno insurance or gift vouchers exist on this screen.
gaps.live_evidence: "Vacante fara griji in 6 rate fara dobanda"; footer links "Vacante in rate / Vouchere vacanta / Asigurare storno / Voucher cadou"
gaps.redesign_evidence: Only `Card de vacanță` filter (Edenred / Pluxee / Up România); no instalment, storno or gift-voucher messaging
gaps.recommendation: Add a compact payment-reassurance strip near the price rail or between result groups: "6 rate fără dobândă · plată cu carduri de vacanță · asigurare storno", each linking to the info page. Consider a per-card "de la X Lei/lună" for high-ticket stays.

gaps.feature: Resort navigation across the whole coast (19 destinations) and the "Hoteluri pe stațiuni" SEO block
gaps.why_it_matters: With one coast and ~500 properties, cross-resort navigation is how LITRO recovers a search that found nothing suitable in Mamaia — and the stațiuni link blocks are significant organic-traffic assets. The redesign offers 3 curated nearby cards, which is nicer to look at but drops 16 destinations and the crawlable link surface. That is a measurable SEO and internal-navigation loss, not a cosmetic one.
gaps.live_evidence: Top strip and footer list 19 resorts (Mamaia, Mamaia Nord, Eforie Nord/Sud, Costinesti, Neptun-Olimp, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Mangalia, Constanta, Vama Veche 2 Mai, Techirghiol, Navodari, Tuzla, Corbu, 23 August, Piatra, Ovidiu); plus "Hoteluri pe statiuni" and "Destinatii populare litoral 2026 / Listă completă de hoteluri" blocks
gaps.redesign_evidence: `near-band` with 3 cards (Mamaia Nord, Eforie Nord, Costinești) and header crumbs only
gaps.recommendation: Keep the 3 editorial near-cards as the visual layer, but restore a full crawlable resort index in the footer and a destination switcher in the header. Confirm the change with SEO before removing the "Hoteluri pe stațiuni" block.

gaps.feature: Campaign landing entry points in navigation (Last minute, Oferta Verii, Litoralul Pentru Toti, Extra Discount, Hoteluri all inclusive) and the second product line (Delta Dunării)
gaps.why_it_matters: These are separately merchandised, separately advertised inventory pools. Losing the nav means paid and returning traffic has no on-site path back into the campaign that brought them, and "Hoteluri all inclusive" in particular is a high-intent, high-ADR entry. Delta Dunării is an entire second destination business that vanishes from the IA.
gaps.live_evidence: Header nav: "Oferte litoral / Last minute / Oferta Verii / WEEKEND NIBIRU / Litoralul Pentru Toti / Extra Discount / Hoteluri all inclusive / Hoteluri Litoral 2026"; "Litoral Romania / Delta Dunarii"
gaps.redesign_evidence: Header contains logo, phone, user chip and a burger icon only — no visible nav items
gaps.recommendation: Define the header nav explicitly in the redesign rather than leaving a burger placeholder: product-line switch (Litoral / Delta Dunării), campaign menu, and account. A hidden nav on desktop at 1440px is an unforced traffic loss.

gaps.feature: Language switcher (EN/RO) and accessibility statement
gaps.why_it_matters: Live serves an English audience and exposes an accessibility page. Dropping the language switch cuts off non-Romanian demand (including diaspora and neighbouring markets) and the accessibility link is increasingly an EAA compliance expectation.
gaps.live_evidence: "LimbaRomână" in header; "Selectati Limba / Engleză Română"; "Accesibilitate"
gaps.redesign_evidence: none found
gaps.recommendation: Add a locale switcher to the header (or user menu) and an accessibility link in the footer legal row.

gaps.feature: Newsletter capture with explicit GDPR consent
gaps.why_it_matters: Email is a cheap re-marketing channel for a seasonal business where the browse-to-book window is weeks long. Live captures on the listing page itself, at the point of highest intent, with a proper consent checkbox.
gaps.live_evidence: "Cele mai bune oferte sunt pe e-mail! Vrei sa le primesti si tu? Inscrie-te acum! / Sunt de acord cu prelucrarea datelor cu caracter personal…"
gaps.redesign_evidence: none found
gaps.recommendation: Add a low-friction capture block after the results (or as an exit-intent), reusing the FRIENDS band styling, with an explicit unticked consent checkbox.

gaps.feature: Editorial property description as fallback for properties without reviews
gaps.why_it_matters: New charter properties launch every season with 0–5 reviews. The live listing handles this gracefully by swapping the review quote for a written description; the redesign has neither, so a new property renders as an empty, untrustworthy card exactly when LITRO most needs to sell it.
gaps.live_evidence: Hotel Albatros, OBA Luxury Boutique, Nicolas House, Beach Vibe Central and Yza Maria all show a description paragraph instead of a review quote ("Hotel Albatros 3*** Mamaia este situat in zona centrala a statiunii…")
gaps.redesign_evidence: none found — no description slot and no zero-review card state
gaps.recommendation: Specify the zero/low-review card state: description snippet in place of the quote, plus a "Nou pe litoralulromanesc.ro" badge so low review counts read as new rather than bad.

gaps.feature: Flexible-dates price strip
gaps.why_it_matters: Worth keeping. Live has nothing equivalent — users who cannot find their price must re-search blindly. Showing ±3 days with a cheapest marker directly monetises charter flexibility and salvages searches that would otherwise bounce on price.
gaps.live_evidence: no equivalent in the live listing
gaps.redesign_evidence: `flexi-strip` with 7 date cells, `cheap` and `sel` states ("3 – 10 iun / 512 Lei / cel mai ieftin")
gaps.recommendation: Keep. Make sure the prices are real availability-backed totals for the same occupancy, otherwise it becomes a trust liability rather than an asset.

gaps.feature: Price histogram with dual-handle range
gaps.why_it_matters: Worth keeping. Replaces live's three coarse buckets (305-687 / 687-1068 / 1068-1452 Lei) with a continuous distribution, which both filters better and communicates where the inventory actually sits — useful when the spread is 2 148 to 12 985 Lei for the same week.
gaps.redesign_evidence: `fbox` "Preț pe noapte" with `histo` bars, `range-line` and Min/Max inputs
gaps.recommendation: Keep. Ensure the axis unit (per night vs per stay) is labelled unambiguously — live prices on cards are stay totals, so a per-night filter against stay-total cards will confuse.

gaps.feature: FRIENDS loyalty band and grouped payment/cancellation filters
gaps.why_it_matters: Worth keeping. Live scatters the loyalty message as a plain-text interstitial ("Stiai ca … credite cadou daca faci rezervarea fiind logat") with no visual weight; the redesign turns it into a proper enrolment CTA. Similarly, live's "Plata la receptie" sits orphaned in the unit-type block while the redesign groups payment and cancellation into one coherent decision set.
gaps.live_evidence: "Stiai ca pentru rezervarile … poti primi credite cadou daca faci rezervarea fiind logat in contul tau de pe site ? / Intra in cont"; "Plata la receptie (7)" under Tipul unitatii
gaps.redesign_evidence: `loyal-band` with "Intră în programul FRIENDS / Înscrie-te gratuit"; `fbox` "Plată și anulare" with Anulare gratuită / Fără plată în avans / Plată online
gaps.recommendation: Keep both. Ensure "Fără plată în avans" maps explicitly to the live "plata la recepție" concept in the copy, since that is the term Romanian users search for.
